_description: Decider mode toggle (PROP-026, landed 2026-05-10). BAU = steady-state with conservative caps. BURNDOWN = elevated caps + tighter safety controls (dry-run-by-default for first 24h, ledger-required) to clear an existing accumulation. Read by decider at Step 0b. See monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-026-open-issues-backlog-clearance.json for full design.
reason: Initial state. Phase 1 (M2 + mode infrastructure + ledger + revert script) deployed; burndown engagement deferred until operator explicitly flips this file to mode='burndown' after reviewing the implementation.
writers: decider (auto-revert path: when count<50 for 3 consecutive runs OR elapsed>7d in burndown OR 5-consecutive-zero-closure runs)

burndown_engagement_protocol.step_1: Operator manually flips mode='burndown', sets reason, sets auto_revert_when_open_below=50 (or chosen threshold), sets auto_revert_after=<+7d ISO> (or chosen elapsed cap).
burndown_engagement_protocol.step_2: Operator also sets dryrun=true on engagement. First 24h M2 produces CANDIDATE-CLOSURE entries to closure-ledger.jsonl with dryrun:true, no actual closures.
burndown_engagement_protocol.step_3: Operator reviews ledger entries after 24h, files HNOTE action='approve_burndown_batch' OR 'cancel_burndown'.
burndown_engagement_protocol.step_4_approve: Decider on next run: dryrun=false, dryrun_approved_at=<now>, dryrun_approved_by=<operator>. Subsequent runs close live with ledger entry.
burndown_engagement_protocol.step_4_cancel: Decider flips mode back to 'bau', dryrun=false. Ledger entries already-written stand as audit trail (dryrun:true clearly marks them as never-closed).
burndown_engagement_protocol.step_5_auto_revert: When auto_revert condition fires: decider sets mode='bau', auto_revert_after=null, auto_revert_when_open_below=null, dryrun=false.
